content.text: Functions are the primary unit of logic in TypeScript. Adding type annotations to parameters and return values catches mismatches at compile time, enables accurate IDE autocomplete, and documents the contract of every function without a comment. This section covers all the ways to declare typed functions and the patterns that appear throughout Angular codebases.

Always annotate the return type of exported functions. For internal helpers where the return type is obvious from context, inference is acceptable — but explicit return types prevent accidental type broadening when the function body changes.
Function expressions assign a function to a variable. Arrow functions (

Default parameters provide a fallback value when the argument is omitted or explicitly passed as undefined.
A rest parameter collects any number of trailing arguments into an array. It must be the last parameter and is typed as
TypeScript supports overload signatures — multiple type signatures for one implementation. Callers see only the overloads; the implementation is hidden.
Functions are first-class values in TypeScript. You can declare a type for a function and use it as a parameter or property type.
Generic functions work with multiple types without losing type information. The type variable is a placeholder resolved at each call site.
TypeScript allows a fake first parameter named
refers to inside the function. This parameter is erased at compile time — it is purely a type annotation.
Typed Functions in Angular Services and Components
Angular uses typed functions throughout its architecture — lifecycle hooks, service methods, event handlers, resolver functions, and guard callbacks all carry explicit type signatures.

Higher-order functions take functions as arguments or return functions. TypeScript infers callback parameter types from the outer function's generic signature.

Pure functions — no side effects, same output for same input — are the easiest to type and test. Angular's service methods and transformation utilities should be pure wherever possible.
Function Signature Best Practices for Angular
Following consistent conventions for function signatures makes Angular codebases easier to navigate and maintain.

TypeScript adds parameter types, return types, optional/default/rest parameters, overloads, and generics to JavaScript functions. These annotations form the function's contract — caught at compile time so runtime surprises are rare.
, or as class methods — all support full type annotations
Overload signatures let callers see clean type contracts while the implementation handles all cases
Generic functions preserve type information through the call — prefer them over any for utilities
In Angular components and services, annotate all public method return types and use arrow functions for callbacks to preserve
